title: pterostylis gibbosa subsp. mitchellii
intro: Pterostylis gibbosa subsp. mitchellii, also known as the Mitchell's greenhood orchid, is a subspecies of terrestrial orchid native to Australia. It is used in traditional medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant's tubers are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although proper identification and preparation are crucial due to potential toxicity.
